services.title: Massage Services
services.subtitle: Services
services.description: Discover our professional massage treatments tailored to your specific needs. From classical relaxation to targeted sports massage – I bring the perfect treatment to your home.

swedish.title: Swedish Massage
swedish.description: Swedish massage is a classic therapeutic treatment aimed at deep relaxation, recovery, and harmonizing the body. Gentle, rhythmic techniques combined with deeper pressure help release accumulated tension, improve circulation, and restore the body's natural state of balance.
swedish.altText: Professional massage therapist performing Swedish massage at home in Zurich

classical.title: Classical Massage
classical.description: Ideal when your body needs rest. Gentle yet deep movements relieve tension, improve circulation, and restore lightness. After a session, you'll feel as if you've shed a few pounds of fatigue – straighter back, freer breathing, brighter mood. Classical massage suits everyone who wants to restore energy and feel the balance between body and mind.
classical.altText: Classical full-body massage with therapeutic techniques to relieve muscle tension

sports.title: Sports Massage
sports.description: For those who live actively: sports, physical work, intensive training. This massage helps muscles recover faster, release tension, regain flexibility and energy. It improves endurance and makes the body more alive and responsive. Ideal before or after workouts – so you don't just train, but enjoy it.
sports.altText: Sports massage for muscle recovery and injury prevention for athletes

anticellulite.title: Anti-Cellulite Massage
anticellulite.description: Not just fighting cellulite – it's a real upgrade for your body. Intensive techniques activate metabolism, improve lymph flow, and remove excess fluid. Skin becomes smoother, firmer, and more alive. After just a few sessions, you'll feel lightness, firmness, and confidence in your body.
anticellulite.altText: Intensive anti-cellulite massage to improve lymph flow and firm skin texture

pregnancy.title: Pregnancy Massage
pregnancy.description: Gentle, caring, safe. Helps relieve tension in back and legs, reduce swelling, and improve sleep. Everything is done gently, in a comfortable position, using natural oils. This massage is not just body care, but a moment of peace and connection with your baby.
pregnancy.altText: Gentle and safe pregnancy massage in comfortable side-lying position at home

children.title: Children's Massage
children.description: Light, kind, and beneficial. Helps children develop muscles, improves sleep, digestion, and mood. Performed in a playful manner – without pain or fear. This massage is not only health care, but also a way to give children a feeling of love and safety.
children.altText: Playful and gentle children's massage supporting development and wellbeing

cupping.title: Cupping Massage
cupping.description: A powerful tool for recovery and vitality. Vacuum cups activate circulation, improve metabolism, and effectively fight cellulite. Also excellent for back pain and muscle tension. After the treatment, the body feels light, and skin becomes firm and elastic.
cupping.altText: Traditional cupping therapy with vacuum cups to promote blood circulation

relaxation.title: Relax Massage
relaxation.description: If you just want to switch off your mind and feel fatigue disappear – this is what you need. Smooth, rhythmic movements, pleasant aromas, and complete relaxation. Just one hour – and the body fills with calm, thoughts become clearer. The perfect way to let go of everything unnecessary and restore inner harmony.
relaxation.altText: Relaxing massage with gentle techniques and warm oils for deep stress relief

pricing.title: Pricing & Terms
pricing.note: Cupping therapy can be added to any massage.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Your Professional Massage Therapist
about.story: My journey to becoming a massage therapist is unique. As a former Olympic gymnast for Ukraine and circus artist, I've spent my life understanding the human body at the highest level. In 2026, I became a participant and prize-winner of the 5th Swiss Massage Championship, taking 1st place in the Swedish Massage category. Through years of athletic performance, I learned firsthand what muscles need to recover, how to prevent injuries, and the importance of proper body care. This deep, personal knowledge of the body's mechanics now informs every massage I give. I bring the discipline of an athlete and the precision of a performer to help you feel pain-free and completely relaxed. Available in German, English, and Russian.

items.question: How does mobile massage work?
items.answer: I come directly to your home with a professional massage table and all necessary equipment. You don't need to prepare anything – just relax in your familiar surroundings.

items.question: What do I need to prepare?
items.answer: You only need 2 large towels and one small towel. I bring the rest: massage table, massage oils, and all necessary materials. You need a room with about 3x3 meters of space.

items.question: How long does a session last?
items.answer: A standard massage session lasts 60 minutes. I also offer extended 90-minute sessions. Additionally, I plan 10-15 minutes for setup and breakdown.

items.question: What equipment do you bring?
items.answer: I bring a professional, adjustable massage table, fresh towels, high-quality massage oils, relaxation music, and cupping glasses if needed. You don't need to provide anything.

items.question: What areas do you serve?
items.answer: I offer mobile massage throughout the Zürichsee region, including Zürich, Schwyz, Luzern, Zug, Aargau, and Rapperswil (St. Gallen). Contact me for specific locations.

items.question: How do I book an appointment?
items.answer: It's easy! Click any 'Book Now' button and you'll be directed to WhatsApp. Alternatively, you can call me at +41 79 849 92 74.

items.question: What's your cancellation policy?
items.answer: I ask for at least 24 hours notice for cancellations. Short-notice cancellations may incur a fee. Rescheduling is usually free of charge.

items.question: Do you have professional certification?
items.answer: Yes! I'm a professionally trained massage therapist. Additionally, I was an Olympic gymnast for Ukraine, which gives me deep understanding of body mechanics and sports injuries.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: I accept cash payment and TWINT. Payment is made after the massage.

items.question: Is it safe to have a massage therapist at home?
items.answer: Absolutely! I'm a professional, certified therapist with years of experience. Many clients appreciate the comfort and privacy of home massage. You can also check my reviews and references.

meta.title: Which Massage is Right for Me? | Guide | olegmassage.com
meta.description: Find the perfect massage for your needs. From stress relief to sports injuries to pregnancy, our guide helps you choose.

whichMassage.title: Which Massage is Right for Me?
whichMassage.description: Every person is different, and every massage has its particular strengths. The right choice depends on your current complaints, your lifestyle and your goals. As the winner of the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I am happy to advise you personally in my practice and help you find the ideal treatment. Read on for an overview of the different options.

stress.title: Feeling stressed and need relaxation?
stress.description: The Relaxation Massage is your ideal companion for stress and mental exhaustion. Flowing, rhythmic movements with pleasant scents release mental tension and give you inner calm. I work with gentle to medium pressure to specifically calm your nervous system. The treatment typically begins in the neck and shoulder area, where stress most commonly accumulates, and then flows over the entire back down to the legs. Essential oils such as lavender or bergamot enhance the relaxing effect and positively influence your mood. After the session, many clients report improved sleep, fewer headaches and a feeling of deep serenity. Perfect after a long work week or during particularly stressful phases of life.

backPain.title: Back pain or muscle tension?
backPain.description: The Classic Massage targets your problem areas precisely and is the most proven method for back pain and tension. Gentle yet deep-reaching techniques such as kneading, stroking and rubbing release tension, improve circulation and bring lightness back. At the beginning of the session, I carefully palpate the affected areas to locate hardening and trigger points. Then I work systematically from the large muscle groups to the specific problem areas. The intensity is individually adjusted to your pain sensitivity. Most clients feel a noticeable improvement in mobility after the first session. For long-term results, I recommend a series of 4-6 treatments at intervals of one to two weeks.

athlete.title: Physically active or athletic?
athlete.description: The Sports Massage supports your recovery and prevents injuries. I use intensive techniques such as deep tissue pressure, stretching grips and cross-fibre friction, specifically tailored to the stressed muscles. Before competitions, the focus is on activation and promoting circulation to boost your performance. After training or competition, I work on reducing lactate accumulation and accelerating muscle recovery. The Sports Massage is particularly effective for recurring problems such as runner's knee, Achilles tendon issues or shoulder tension. I adapt every treatment to your sport and training programme. Many athletes in the Schwyz region trust me as their regular massage therapist.

skin.title: Want to improve your skin texture?
skin.description: The Anti-Cellulite Massage activates metabolism, improves lymph flow and firms the skin. Through special techniques such as rhythmic kneading, vacuum effects and targeted pressure movements, microcirculation in the affected tissue areas is significantly increased. The treatment focuses on the typical problem areas: thighs, buttocks and abdomen. Waste products are released and their removal via the lymphatic system is stimulated. Visible results appear after a regular series of 6-10 sessions. The skin looks firmer, smoother and better supplied with blood. For optimal results, I recommend drinking plenty of water and incorporating light exercise into your daily routine.

pregnant.title: Expecting a baby?
pregnant.description: The Pregnancy Massage is safe from the second trimester onwards and helps with back pain, swelling and sleep problems. Everything is carried out gently in a comfortable side-lying position with special positioning cushions, so you and your baby are safe and comfortable at all times. The focus is on relieving the lower back, legs and shoulders - the areas most stressed during pregnancy. Gentle strokes and light pressure promote lymphatic drainage and can noticeably reduce water retention in the legs. Certain body areas and pressure points are deliberately avoided to ensure the safety of mother and child. Many expectant mothers report that they can finally sleep restfully again after the massage. The treatment can be carried out until shortly before the due date.

child.title: Your child needs relaxation?
child.description: The Children's Massage is carried out in a playful and caring way and is suitable for children from preschool age. It supports physical and emotional development, improves sleep and gives your child a sense of security. The sessions are shorter than for adults, typically 20-30 minutes, and are adapted to your child's age and needs. I use gentle, child-friendly techniques and skin-compatible oils without strong fragrances. The Children's Massage is particularly helpful for concentration difficulties, restlessness or growing pains. A parent is present throughout the entire treatment so your child feels safe. Many parents observe positive changes in sleep behaviour and overall balance in their child after regular sessions.

chronic.title: Chronic pain issues?
chronic.description: The Cupping Massage intensively activates circulation and releases even deep tension and trigger points. By applying special cupping glasses, a negative pressure is created that separates the tissue layers and increases local circulation many times over. This technique is particularly effective for chronic pain in the neck, shoulder and back area. The treatment can be booked as a standalone therapy or as a complement to classic massage. After the session, temporary round skin discolourations may appear, which fade completely within a few days. Many clients with long-standing pain conditions report significant and lasting improvement after a cupping massage series. I often combine the cupping technique with manual massage to achieve optimal results.

premium.title: Looking for the best massage experience?
premium.description: The Swedish Massage combines deep relaxation with therapeutic effect and is my speciality. Awarded 1st place at the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I offer you the highest level of massage artistry here. This technique combines five basic strokes: Effleurage (stroking), Petrissage (kneading), Friction (rubbing), Tapotement (tapping) and Vibration. Each stroke is applied precisely and with great experience to achieve both relaxing and therapeutic effects. The Swedish Massage is suitable for almost everyone, whether you are seeking relaxation, pain relief or general health improvement. A typical session lasts 60 or 90 minutes and covers the entire body. If you are unsure which massage is right for you, the Swedish Massage is always an excellent choice.

howToChoose.title: How do I choose the right massage?
howToChoose.description: Choosing the right massage begins with an honest assessment of your current situation. Ask yourself: What is my main concern? Is it about relaxation, pain relief, athletic performance or aesthetic goals? If you are unsure, a short conversation via WhatsApp is often the best first step. I will ask you targeted questions about your complaints, your lifestyle and your expectations. Based on that, I recommend the appropriate massage type and the optimal treatment duration. Keep in mind that your needs may change over time. An athlete needs a different massage during the competition phase than during the recovery phase. Don't hesitate to try different massage types, because often only in practice does it become clear which technique works best for you.

combination.title: Combining massage types
combination.description: In many cases, a combination of different massage techniques is the most effective approach. For example, with clients who have back pain and high stress levels, I often start with relaxing strokes from the Relaxation Massage before addressing the specific problem areas with deeper techniques from the Classic Massage. For athletes, I combine Sports Massage with cupping to release particularly stubborn tension. The Swedish Massage can also be excellently complemented with targeted anti-cellulite strokes if you want both relaxation and skin firming at the same time. In a personal consultation beforehand, we determine together which combinations make the most sense for your situation. The advantage of my mobile practice is that I can respond flexibly to your needs without being bound to a rigid treatment scheme. This way, you receive a tailored treatment every time.

meta.title: Your First Massage, What to Expect | Guide | olegmassage.com
meta.description: Everything you need to know before your first mobile massage. From booking to the session, explained step by step.

firstMassage.title: Your First Massage
firstMassage.description: You have never had a mobile massage before? No problem - here you will learn step by step how everything works. From the first contact to aftercare, I guide you through the entire process so you feel comfortable from the start and know what to expect.

booking.title: 1. Book via WhatsApp
booking.description: Simply send me a message via WhatsApp. We discuss your wishes, choose the appropriate massage and schedule an appointment that fits your timetable, including evenings and weekends. I am happy to answer all your questions about the treatment, contraindications or pricing. If you are not yet sure which massage is right for you, I advise you without obligation. Appointments can usually be arranged within a few days, often even at short notice. You will receive an appointment confirmation via WhatsApp and a reminder the day before. Should you need to cancel or reschedule an appointment, this is possible free of charge up to 24 hours beforehand.

preparation.title: 2. Prepare at Home
preparation.description: You only need 2 large and 1 small towel plus a room of about 3x3 metres. I bring the rest: professional massage table, high-quality oils and relaxing music. The room should ideally be quiet, warm and well ventilated. A bedroom or living room is perfect for this. Set the room temperature to about 22-24 degrees so you don't get cold during the massage. If you prefer certain oils or scents, or have allergies, please let me know in advance. It is advisable not to eat a heavy meal 1-2 hours before the massage. A glass of water before the treatment helps your body respond optimally to the massage.

arrival.title: 3. Setup and Welcome
arrival.description: I arrive 10-15 minutes before the appointment to set everything up. The professional massage table is ready in just a few minutes and is fitted with fresh covers. We briefly discuss your current complaints, wishes and any limitations. This preliminary conversation is important so I can tailor the treatment precisely to you. I ask about problem areas, pain sensitivity and whether there are areas that should be avoided. Information about pre-existing conditions, surgeries or pregnancy is also relevant for a safe treatment. Everything you share with me is treated with strict confidentiality.

during.title: 4. During the Massage
during.description: Relax completely. The massage lasts 60 or 90 minutes. You can give feedback at any time - more or less pressure, emphasise or skip certain areas. During the treatment, only the body areas currently being massaged are uncovered. Everything else is draped with towels so you feel comfortable and protected at all times. I usually work with relaxing background music, which you can also have turned off if you prefer silence. You don't have to talk during the massage, but you are welcome to. Many clients even fall asleep, and that is a good sign. I continuously adjust the course of the treatment to your reactions.

after.title: 5. After the Massage
after.description: Drink plenty of water and allow yourself to rest. Payment is conveniently made in cash or via TWINT. I pack everything up and leave your room just as I found it. Take at least 15-20 minutes after the massage before becoming active again. Your body needs a moment to process the effects of the treatment. Avoid intensive physical activity and hot baths on the same day. Drink plenty of water for the rest of the day to support the removal of released metabolic by-products. If you have questions or notice unusual sensations the next day, you can contact me anytime via WhatsApp.

whatToWear.title: What should I wear?
whatToWear.description: You don't need to prepare any special clothing for the massage. You can wear comfortable everyday clothes that you simply remove before the treatment. During the massage, most clients wear only underwear. You are always covered with towels, and only the body area currently being treated is uncovered. If you feel more comfortable, you can also keep on shorts or a top. Please avoid applying body lotion or perfume before the massage, as these can interfere with the effectiveness of the massage oils. Jewellery such as necklaces, watches or bracelets should be removed beforehand so the massage can flow without interruption.

commonQuestions.title: Common questions from first-time visitors
commonQuestions.description: Many people wonder before their first massage: Does it hurt? With a Relaxation or Swedish Massage, the pressure is pleasant and relaxing. With therapeutic massages, a slight pulling sensation may occur, but it should never be uncomfortable. Another common question: Do I have to make conversation? No, you may close your eyes and switch off completely. Others ask whether massage is safe with certain health conditions. Generally, there are few contraindications - these include fever, acute inflammation and certain skin conditions. If you are unsure, we clarify this in the preliminary conversation. Some first-time visitors also worry about hygiene with a mobile massage. I always use fresh covers, disinfect my equipment after every treatment and wash my hands thoroughly. Your health and well-being always come first.

afterEffects.title: What to expect in the days afterwards
afterEffects.description: In the 24-48 hours after your first massage, various reactions may occur that are completely normal. Mild muscle soreness, similar to after a workout, is especially possible after more intensive treatments. Some people feel particularly tired on the evening of the massage and sleep more deeply than usual. Others report a surge of energy the next morning. Increased urination may also occur, as the massage stimulates lymph flow and metabolic by-products are released. Therefore, drink especially plenty of water in the following days. Occasionally, existing pain may briefly intensify before a noticeable improvement occurs. If unusual or persistent complaints arise, contact me via WhatsApp. From the second or third session, your body already knows the treatment, and the after-effects become more pleasant while the positive effects grow stronger.

meta.title: Mobile Massage vs. Studio, Advantages Compared | olegmassage.com
meta.description: Why is mobile massage better than visiting a studio? No stress, no commute, no parking. Relaxation starts right at your home.

mobileVsStudio.title: Mobile Massage vs. Studio
mobileVsStudio.description: Why are more and more people choosing mobile massage instead of visiting a studio? The trend towards mobile massage is growing steadily in Switzerland. The reasons are obvious: more comfort, less stress and deeper relaxation. Here are the most important differences in detail.

mobile.title: Mobile Massage
mobile.1: No commute, I come to your home
mobile.2: No stress with parking or public transport
mobile.3: Relaxation starts immediately in familiar surroundings
mobile.4: Flexible scheduling, including evenings and weekends
mobile.5: After the massage, go straight to your own bed or sofa
mobile.6: Professional equipment, same quality as a studio

studio.title: Studio Massage
studio.1: Travel to and from takes time and energy
studio.2: Parking hassles or waiting for public transport
studio.4: Relaxation often fades on the way home

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Mobile massage saves you time, stress and lets the relaxation last much longer. You don't have to worry about anything - I bring everything and leave your room just as I found it. As a prize winner of the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I guarantee you professional quality at the highest level. Especially for people with a full schedule, families with small children or persons with limited mobility, mobile massage is a great benefit. The relaxation begins in your familiar surroundings and does not end abruptly at the studio door. Instead, you can let the soothing effect seamlessly flow into your evening.

costComparison.title: Price-performance comparison
costComparison.description: At first glance, a mobile massage may cost slightly more than a comparable studio treatment. However, when you factor in the hidden costs of a studio visit, the difference quickly puts things in perspective. Travel costs for car or public transport, parking fees, the time spent on the journey there and back, and possibly even a babysitter all add up. With a mobile massage, you pay a transparent price and save 30-60 minutes of travel time at the same time. You can use this gained time for yourself, for example for a walk, time with the family or simply resting. Many of my regular clients say that mobile massage offers better value for money when they consider the total effort involved.

whoIsItFor.title: Who is mobile massage particularly suited for?
whoIsItFor.description: Mobile massage is ideal for working professionals who have no energy left for a trip to the studio after a long working day. Young parents also benefit enormously, as the massage can take place while the child is sleeping or being looked after by a partner. Older people or persons with limited mobility don't have to struggle with stairs, car parks or public transport. Couples can be treated one after another and let the evening wind down together. Mobile massage is also an attractive option for companies: employee massages directly in the office boost satisfaction and reduce absence. And finally, athletes also appreciate the possibility of being treated directly at home after training, without interrupting the recovery effect with a car journey.

quality.title: Equipment and quality standards
quality.description: A common prejudice against mobile massage is that the quality cannot match a studio. The opposite is the case. I use a professional, height-adjustable massage table that offers the same comfort as the tables in renowned practices. My high-quality massage oils are dermatologically tested and suited to different skin types. Fresh covers and towels are a matter of course with every treatment. Relaxing music from a portable speaker rounds off the atmosphere. As the winner of the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I bring not only first-class equipment but also the expertise that has been tested at the highest competitive level. Every treatment follows strict hygiene standards, and the equipment is thoroughly cleaned and disinfected after each session.

meta.title: How Often Should You Get a Massage? | Optimal Frequency | olegmassage.com
meta.description: How often should you get a massage? The ideal frequency depends on your goals: stress relief, sports recovery, pain management, or prevention.

massageFrequency.title: How Often Should You Get a Massage?
massageFrequency.intro: The ideal massage frequency depends on your personal goals and life situation. There is no universal answer, but there are proven guidelines based on years of practical experience. Here you will find specific recommendations for different life situations.

general.title: General Recommendation
general.description: For most people, a massage every 2-4 weeks is ideal for preventing tension and promoting general well-being. Regularity is more important than frequency - your body gets used to the treatment and benefits more from it in the long run. Think of massage like exercise: jogging once a month produces less benefit than regular, moderate training. The body builds on the effects of each previous session. Tension resolves more sustainably, the muscles stay more supple, and the nervous system learns to switch into relaxation mode more quickly. If you are just starting with regular massages, I recommend a bi-weekly rhythm for the first two months. After that, many clients can switch to a monthly schedule and maintain the improved quality of life.

stress.title: For Stress and Burnout Prevention
stress.description: If stress dominates your daily life, I recommend weekly sessions initially for 4-6 weeks. After that, you can reduce to every 2 weeks. The Relaxation Massage and Swedish Massage are particularly effective here - they have been shown to lower cortisol levels and promote the release of endorphins. With chronic stress, the body enters a permanent state of alert, leading to sleep disorders, digestive problems and muscle tension. Regular massages help break this cycle. After just a few sessions, many clients report better sleep, fewer headaches and greater resilience in daily life. Long-term, a rhythm of every 2-3 weeks is recommended to keep stress levels at a healthy level. In addition to massage, breathing exercises and short stretching breaks during the day can also be helpful.

sport.title: For Athletes and Active People
sport.description: Regularly training athletes benefit from weekly sports massages during intensive training phases. Before competitions, I recommend a session 2-3 days beforehand; after intensive exertion, within 24-48 hours. During rest phases, every 2-3 weeks is sufficient. The Sports Massage measurably shortens recovery time by promoting circulation and accelerating the removal of metabolic by-products. For endurance athletes like runners or cyclists, I focus particularly on the leg muscles and hip flexors. Strength athletes benefit from targeted treatment of shoulders, back and arms. Important: Immediately after a competition, a gentler massage is appropriate than during the regular training rhythm. Listen to your body and adjust the frequency to your training intensity. During pre-season preparation, a higher frequency may be beneficial, while in the off-season, a relaxed maintenance massage is sufficient.

pain.title: For Chronic Pain
pain.description: For chronic back or neck pain, we start with 2 sessions per week for 3-4 weeks. Once the pain subsides, we gradually reduce to weekly and then to every 2 weeks. Cupping massage can be used as an effective complement here. Chronic pain often results from an interplay of muscle tension, poor posture and psychological stress. A single massage can bring short-term relief, but for lasting results, a consistent treatment plan is crucial. During the intensive phase, I work specifically on the pain causes: shortened muscles are stretched, trigger points are deactivated and adhesions in the fascia are released. At the same time, I show you simple exercises for home that support the treatment success between sessions. Many clients reach a pain level after 6-8 weeks of intensive treatment that can be maintained with a monthly maintenance massage.

pregnancy.title: During Pregnancy
pregnancy.description: From the second trimester, I recommend a gentle pregnancy massage every 2-3 weeks. In the third trimester, weekly massage can be given if needed. The massage is individually adjusted to the respective phase. In the second trimester, the focus is often on preventing back pain and promoting circulation. In the third trimester, relieving swollen legs and easing pelvic and lumbar pain are the priorities. Many expectant mothers report that regular massages also improve their emotional balance and reduce fears about the birth. After delivery, a postnatal massage at intervals of 2-3 weeks can support the healing process. Please also discuss your massage plan with your midwife or gynaecologist, especially in high-risk pregnancies.

maintenance.title: For Prevention and Self-Care
maintenance.description: Even without acute complaints, a monthly massage is an excellent investment in your health. It prevents tension, improves sleep quality and strengthens the immune system. Many of my regular clients have been coming for years and report significantly fewer sick days. Preventive massage is comparable to regular dental cleaning: it doesn't wait for a problem but prevents it. People who sit at a desk a lot particularly benefit from a monthly treatment that relieves the neck, shoulders and lower back. Mental health also benefits, because one hour of conscious relaxation acts like a reset for body and mind. In the long run, prevention saves you not only medical costs but also gains you quality of life. I recommend my regular clients schedule fixed monthly appointments so that massage becomes a permanent part of their self-care routine.

signs.title: Recognising your body's signals
signs.description: Your body sends clear signals when it needs a massage. Frequent headaches, especially in the temple or back of the head area, often indicate neck tension. A stiff neck in the morning, despite a good mattress, is a clear sign of muscular overload. If you feel a pulling sensation when turning your head or your shoulders are constantly raised, it is high time for a treatment. Sleep disturbances, a restless mind and a feeling of inner tension that you cannot let go of are also signs that your nervous system needs a break. Tingling or numbness in the arms may indicate tension in the shoulder-neck area that is impairing nerve flow. Pay attention to these signals and don't wait until minor complaints become chronic problems. Early and regular massage is the best way to keep your body healthy and performing well.

longTerm.title: Long-term benefits of regular massage
longTerm.description: The true benefits of regular massage only reveal themselves over months and years. Studies confirm that regular massage can lower blood pressure, strengthen the immune system and sustainably improve sleep quality. People who receive regular massages report better body awareness and respond more quickly to early signs of overload. The muscles remain more supple long-term and the fascia more elastic, promoting mobility well into old age. The psychological effects are also considerable: less anxiety, better mood and greater stress resilience. Regular massage can also reduce the frequency of tension headaches and migraines. Many of my long-standing clients describe regular massage as a fixed anchor in their daily life that helps them cope better with life's demands. Investing in regular massage is an investment in your long-term health and quality of life.

meta.title: Does Massage Help with Back Pain? | Guide | olegmassage.com
meta.description: Learn how massage helps with back pain. Which massage types are most effective and how often you should get treated.

backPain.title: Does Massage Help with Back Pain?
backPain.intro: Back pain is the most common cause of work absences in Switzerland. Around 80 percent of all adults suffer from back pain at least once in their lives, and for many, the complaints become chronic. Massage is one of the most effective non-pharmaceutical treatments for it. As a mobile massage therapist in the Zurich Lake region, I treat clients with all kinds of back problems every day. The results are often noticeable after the very first session. In this guide, you will learn which types of massage best help with back pain, how often you should have treatment and what you can do yourself.

causes.title: Common Causes of Back Pain
causes.description: Prolonged sitting at the office, lack of exercise, poor posture, stress and one-sided strain - these are the most common triggers. Muscles shorten and harden, fascia become adhesive, and the pain becomes chronic. Massage breaks this vicious cycle. Office workers who spend eight hours or more per day in front of the computer are particularly affected. The back muscles are continuously strained one-sidedly, while the stabilising abdominal muscles weaken. Psychological stress also plays a major role: under tension, many people unconsciously raise their shoulders and tense the neck muscles. Added to this are factors like incorrect lifting, a soft mattress or missing ergonomic adjustments at the workplace. All these causes lead to muscular imbalances that continue to worsen without treatment.

whichMassage.title: Which Massage Works Best?
whichMassage.description: The choice of the right massage depends on the type and intensity of your back pain. Here is an overview of the most effective options. For mild to moderate complaints, the Classic Massage is the ideal starting point. For deep-seated, chronic tension, the Sports Massage is better suited. For particularly stubborn problem areas, the Cupping Massage complements the treatment optimally. In an initial consultation, I analyse your complaints and recommend the right combination. Often a mix of different techniques is most effective, adapted to the course of each session.

classical.title: Classical Massage for General Back Pain
classical.description: The Classic Massage is the all-rounder for back pain. It releases tension throughout the back muscles, improves circulation and promotes recovery. Ideal for mild to moderate complaints and as regular prevention. The treatment begins with gentle stroking movements that warm the tissue and stimulate circulation. Then I use kneading and rubbing techniques to target tense areas. The pressure is gradually increased to reach deeper muscle layers as well. The Classic Massage is particularly effective for tension in the upper back, the shoulder-neck area and along the spine. Many clients report noticeable relief and improved mobility after just one session. With regular application, the Classic Massage prevents new tension and keeps the muscles supple.

sports.title: Sports Massage for Deep-Seated Tension
sports.description: When surface-level techniques are not enough, the Sports Massage comes into play. With targeted deep tissue pressure and specialised grips, even stubborn knots and adhesions are released. Particularly effective for pain between the shoulder blades and in the lower back. The Sports Massage works more intensively than the Classic Massage and reaches the deep muscle layers that are often affected in chronic complaints. Techniques such as trigger point treatment, myofascial release techniques and cross-fibre friction are used. The pressure is more noticeable than with a Classic Massage but always remains within a therapeutically appropriate range. After a Sports Massage, mild muscle soreness may occur, similar to after an intensive workout. This subsides within one to two days and is a sign that deep tension has been released. For athletes and physically active people, the Sports Massage is often the first choice for back pain.

cupping.title: Cupping Massage for Chronic Issues
cupping.description: Cupping massage creates strong negative pressure that massively increases circulation in the affected areas. Particularly effective for chronic complaints that only partially respond to regular massage. It can be added as a complement to any massage. The cupping glasses are placed on the back and create a vacuum that lifts the skin and underlying tissue. This effect releases fascial adhesions, which are often a main cause of chronic back pain. The increased circulation brings fresh nutrients to the tissue and accelerates the removal of metabolic waste products. After the treatment, circular reddening may appear on the skin, which disappears on its own after a few days. Especially for clients who have had back pain for months and for whom classic massage techniques alone are insufficient, the cupping massage often provides the decisive breakthrough.

frequency.title: How Often for Back Pain?
frequency.description: For acute complaints, I recommend 2 sessions per week for 2-3 weeks. For chronic pain, a long-term approach with weekly sessions over 6-8 weeks is more effective. For prevention, one session every 2-4 weeks is then sufficient. The optimal frequency depends on the severity and duration of your complaints. Clients with acute back pain, for example after a strain, often feel noticeable improvement after just 3-4 intensive sessions. For chronic complaints that have persisted for months or years, the body needs more time for recovery. Patience is required here, but the results are more lasting. Many of my clients stay on a regular schedule of one massage per month after the acute treatment to remain pain-free. This preventive approach is more effective and less costly long-term than repeated acute treatments.

prevention.title: Preventing Back Pain
prevention.description: Regular massage combined with exercise is the best protection against back pain. Stand up briefly every hour, stretch regularly and invest in an ergonomic workplace. A monthly massage keeps your muscles supple and prevents adhesions. Pay attention to balanced posture in daily life, whether sitting, standing or walking. Strengthen your core muscles specifically through regular training, as strong abdominal and back muscles stabilise the spine. Avoid lifting heavy objects with a rounded back - go into your knees instead. Stress management also contributes to back health, because psychological tension leads directly to muscle tension. Try relaxation techniques such as conscious breathing or short breaks during the working day.

exercises.title: Complementary exercises and stretches
exercises.description: Targeted exercises between massage appointments significantly enhance the therapeutic effect. I recommend my clients a simple routine of 10-15 minutes daily. Start with the cat-cow exercise: on all fours, alternately round your back and arch it, 10 repetitions each. This exercise mobilises the entire spine and releases light tension. The child's pose (sitting on your heels with arms stretched forward on the floor) gently stretches the lower back and lateral trunk muscles. Hold the position for 30 seconds. For the shoulder-neck area, lateral head tilts and slow shoulder circles are suitable. The chest stretch in the doorframe is also very effective against the typical office worker posture with shoulders pulled forward. Regularity is key: short daily stretching sessions are more effective than infrequent long training sessions. I am happy to show you the exercises suited to your complaints at your next appointment.

ergonomics.title: Workplace ergonomics
ergonomics.description: An ergonomically set up workplace can massively reduce or even completely prevent back pain. The top of the screen should be at eye level so you don't have to lower or raise your head. The rule of thumb for sitting posture is 90-90-90: hips, knees and ankles each form a right angle. Invest in a good office chair with an adjustable lumbar support that follows the natural curve of the lumbar spine. Even better is a height-adjustable desk where you can alternate between sitting and standing. A switch every 30-45 minutes is recommended. The position of the keyboard and mouse is also important: your forearms should rest comfortably and your shoulders should not be raised. Anyone who talks on the phone a lot should use a headset instead of clamping the phone between ear and shoulder. Even small adjustments can make a big difference. Combined with regular massage and exercise, an ergonomic workplace forms the foundation for a pain-free back.

whenToSeeDoctor.title: When you should see a doctor
whenToSeeDoctor.description: Massage is an excellent treatment for most back pain. However, there are warning signs where you should see a doctor first. Seek medical help if the pain occurred after an accident or fall. Radiating pain into the legs, tingling, numbness or loss of strength in the legs are signs of possible nerve involvement and should be medically examined. Pain that worsens at night or wakes you from sleep may point to more serious causes. Further warning signs include fever combined with back pain, unintentional weight loss or problems with urination. If your back pain does not improve despite regular massage treatment over 4-6 weeks, I also recommend a medical evaluation. In most cases, the doctor gives the all-clear after examination and recommends massage as part of the therapy. Don't hesitate to see a doctor one time too many rather than too few in case of doubt.

meta.title: Swedish vs. Classical Massage, The Difference | olegmassage.com
meta.description: What's the difference between Swedish and Classical massage? Techniques, effects, and which massage is better suited for you.

swedishVsClassical.title: Swedish vs. Classical Massage, The Difference
swedishVsClassical.intro: Both massage types are therapeutically valuable but differ in technique, purpose and effect. As the winner of the Swiss Massage Championship 2026 in the Swedish Massage category, I explain the differences. Many clients ask me which of the two massage types is better suited for them. The answer depends on your individual needs, complaints and preferences. In this guide, you will learn the key differences, similarities and which massage is the better choice for which situations. Both forms have their merits and can complement each other excellently.

overview.title: Similarities and Differences
overview.description: Both massage types use oil and work with the entire body. The main difference lies in the technique: Swedish Massage uses five specific stroke techniques in a set sequence, while Classic Massage is more flexible in addressing individual problem areas. Both massages promote circulation, release muscle tension and support the body's recovery. Swedish Massage follows a structured protocol and treats the entire body in a systematic order. Classic Massage, on the other hand, allows the therapist to respond spontaneously to the client's needs and spend more time on particularly affected areas. Both massages can be booked as 60 or 90-minute sessions. In practice, the techniques of both massage types overlap considerably, though the weighting and application differ.

swedish.title: What Makes Swedish Massage Special?
swedish.description: Swedish Massage was developed in the 19th century by Per Henrik Ling and is the most widely practised massage form in the world. It combines Effleurage (stroking), Petrissage (kneading), Friction (rubbing), Tapotement (tapping) and Vibration in a harmonious sequence. The result is deep relaxation with simultaneous therapeutic effect. Each of the five techniques has a specific function: Effleurage warms the tissue and calms the nervous system, Petrissage loosens the muscles, Friction releases adhesions in deeper tissue layers, Tapotement stimulates circulation and invigorates the muscles, Vibration has a decramp and pain-relieving effect. The harmonious alternation of these techniques creates an overall experience that alleviates physical complaints while simultaneously promoting deep mental relaxation. Swedish Massage is therefore particularly suitable for people seeking both physical and psychological relief.

classical.title: What Makes Classical Massage Special?
classical.description: Classic Massage is the European approach that is more strongly oriented towards medical principles. It is more flexible in the choice of techniques and focuses specifically on the areas that need the most attention. It is often recommended by doctors and physiotherapists. The therapist selects from a broad repertoire of techniques those that are most effective for the particular client and their complaints. This means that two sessions with the same therapist may well proceed differently, depending on the client's current condition. Classic Massage can be focused locally on a specific body region, for example only on the neck-shoulder area or the lower back. This flexibility makes it particularly valuable for specific complaints and in the rehabilitation area. In Switzerland, Classic Massage is the most commonly prescribed massage form by doctors.

techniques.title: Techniques Compared
techniques.description: Swedish Massage follows a systematic full-body protocol - each area is treated in a specific order. Classic Massage is more adaptive: I can spend more time on problem areas and adjust techniques spontaneously. Both use similar basic strokes but apply them differently. In Swedish Massage, the sequence of techniques and body regions is fixed, creating a rhythmic, flowing progression. The transitions between techniques are smooth and harmonious, which contributes to deep relaxation. In Classic Massage, I can for example treat only the neck for 20 minutes if the main complaints are located there. The intensity also varies: while Swedish Massage tends to use consistent pressure, Classic Massage can range from very gentle to intensive. In practice, I combine my knowledge and experience in both forms to achieve the best result for each client.

whenSwedish.title: When Is Swedish Massage the Better Choice?
whenSwedish.description: Choose Swedish Massage if you are looking for a holistic treatment that addresses body and mind equally. Ideal for stress reduction, general recovery, improving sleep quality and as a regular wellness ritual. Particularly recommended for massage newcomers. Swedish Massage is the best choice when you have a demanding daily life and simply want to switch off. Thanks to the systematic full-body protocol, no area is neglected - you receive a complete treatment from head to toe. Many of my clients choose Swedish Massage as a regular monthly ritual for their health maintenance. Also for diffuse complaints, when you cannot pinpoint exactly where it hurts, Swedish Massage is ideal. The deep relaxation it creates helps the body activate its own healing processes.

whenClassical.title: When Is Classical Massage the Better Choice?
whenClassical.description: Choose Classic Massage if you want to work specifically on certain complaints, for example tension in the neck, pain in the lower back or restricted mobility. Here I can focus the treatment more strongly on your specific problems. Classic Massage is particularly effective for concrete, localisable complaints. When you know exactly that your neck is tense or your lower back hurts, I can concentrate the entire session on these areas. After sports injuries or during rehabilitation measures, Classic Massage is often the better choice. For clients who already have massage experience and want to address specific problems, Classic Massage offers the greatest benefit. In combination with medical therapy or physiotherapy, it is a valuable component of the treatment plan.

verdict.title: My Recommendation
verdict.description: Both massages are outstanding. If you are unsure, start with Swedish Massage - it offers the best overall experience and the deepest relaxation. For targeted complaints, we then switch to Classic Massage. As the winner of the Swiss Massage Championship in the Swedish Massage category, I can offer both forms at the highest level. In practice, I recommend many of my clients alternate between both massage types. For example, a Swedish Massage as a regular relaxation ritual and a Classic Massage when specific complaints arise. This way, you benefit from the strengths of both approaches. Some clients discover after their first Swedish Massage that they want to work on specific areas and then switch to Classic Massage. Others stay with Swedish Massage because the holistic experience appeals to them most. There is no wrong choice - both massages are therapeutically effective and beneficial.

history.title: History and origins
history.description: Swedish Massage dates back to Per Henrik Ling (1776-1839), a Swedish gymnastics teacher and fencer. Ling developed a system of medical gymnastics that also included massage techniques. His student Johan Georg Mezger (1838-1909), a Dutch physician, systematised the techniques and gave them the French names still used today: Effleurage, Petrissage, Friction, Tapotement and Vibration. Classic Massage has its roots in European medical tradition and developed over centuries from various healing practices. Already in antiquity, Greek and Roman physicians used massage techniques to treat injuries and diseases. In the 19th and 20th centuries, Classic Massage was further developed and standardised in Central Europe as a medical therapy form. Interestingly, both traditions influenced each other. Swedish Massage adopted elements from the European medical tradition, while Classic Massage benefited from Ling's systematic approach. Today, both massage types are recognised worldwide and taught in professional training programmes.

duration.title: Recommended session duration
duration.description: The optimal session duration differs depending on the massage type and objectives. For Swedish Massage, I generally recommend 90 minutes. The full-body protocol needs sufficient time to thoroughly treat each area and fully develop the relaxing effect. A 60-minute session is also possible but requires adjustments to the procedure, with some areas being treated for less time. For Classic Massage, 60 minutes is often sufficient, especially when the focus is on one or two problem areas. Those who want a more comprehensive treatment also benefit from 90 minutes here. For acute complaints, a focused 60-minute Classic Massage can be more effective than a longer session, as the concentration on the problem area is at its maximum. My tip: for the first appointment, choose 90 minutes, regardless of the massage type. This gives us enough time for a thorough conversation, the complete treatment and a brief follow-up discussion. For subsequent appointments, you can then choose between 60 and 90 minutes depending on your needs.

meta.title: Massage During Pregnancy, When Is It Safe? | olegmassage.com
meta.description: Everything about massage during pregnancy: when it's safe, the benefits, which techniques are used. Professional prenatal massage at your home.

pregnancyMassage.title: Massage During Pregnancy, When Is It Safe?
pregnancyMassage.intro: Massage during pregnancy is safe from the second trimester and offers numerous benefits for mother and child. Here you will find everything you need to know. Pregnancy brings wonderful changes but also physical challenges such as back pain, swollen legs and sleep problems. A professional pregnancy massage can significantly alleviate these complaints and contribute to overall well-being. As a mobile massage therapist, I come directly to your home, which is a great advantage especially during pregnancy.

safety.title: When Is Prenatal Massage Safe?
safety.description: Pregnancy massage is safe from week 13 (start of the second trimester). In the first trimester, massage is avoided as a precaution. For high-risk pregnancies, you should consult your doctor or midwife beforehand. The caution in the first trimester is purely a precautionary measure, as the risk of complications is generally highest during this phase. There is no scientific evidence that gentle massage is harmful in the first trimester. Nevertheless, I follow the principle of caution. From week 13, the pregnancy is more stable and the massage can be carried out without concern. I adjust the pressure, techniques and positioning throughout the entire treatment to your pregnancy week. Before each session, I have a brief conversation about your current health status and any new complaints.

benefits.title: Benefits of Prenatal Massage
benefits.description: Pregnancy massage alleviates back pain (the most common complaint), reduces swelling in legs and feet, improves sleep quality, lowers stress hormones and promotes emotional connection with the baby. Studies also show a reduction in anxiety and depression. The hormonal changes during the massage are measurable: cortisol levels (stress hormone) decrease, while serotonin and dopamine (feel-good hormones) increase. This effect lasts several days after the massage. For the baby, less stress in the mother means a calmer environment and better nourishment. Many pregnant women also report that the massage helps them find a more conscious connection to their changing body. The improved circulation also supports the baby's nutrient supply and can noticeably reduce water retention.

trimester.title: Massage by Trimester
trimester.description: In the second trimester, the focus is on alleviating back pain and muscle tension. In the third trimester, we concentrate on swollen legs, hip pain and general relaxation. The massage is performed in a comfortable side-lying position, supported by special cushions. In the second trimester, the belly grows visibly and the body begins to adapt: posture changes, the chest becomes heavier and the lower back is more heavily strained. The massage focuses here on the lumbar area, the shoulder-neck area and the leg muscles. In the third trimester, complaints often increase: the hip joints widen, the baby's weight presses on the pelvic area, and water retention can cause legs and feet to swell. Here I use gentle lymphatic drainage techniques to reduce swelling, and I focus on the lower back and hip muscles. The pressure is adjusted to be increasingly gentle as the pregnancy progresses.

techniques.title: Which Techniques Are Used?
techniques.description: I use exclusively gentle techniques with light to medium pressure. Certain pressure points and areas are deliberately avoided. Only natural oils without artificial additives are used. The entire treatment is designed to provide maximum relaxation with complete safety. Long, flowing stroking movements are primarily used, which have a calming effect and promote circulation. Kneading techniques are applied only lightly to loosen tense muscles in the back and shoulder area. Deep tissue work and strong pressure are avoided during pregnancy. Certain areas such as the abdomen, the inner ankles and specific acupressure points are avoided, as these could trigger contractions. The oils used are purely natural and free from essential oils that are not recommended during pregnancy. On request, I also use fragrance-neutral oils, as many pregnant women have heightened sensitivity to smells.

contraindications.title: When Should You Avoid Massage?
contraindications.description: Massage is not recommended in cases of: bleeding, preeclampsia, placenta praevia, premature contractions, fever or acute infections. If you are unsure, discuss it with your doctor. I ask about the current health status before each treatment. Further contraindications include severe nausea with vomiting, dizziness, unusual swelling (especially in the face and hands, which may indicate preeclampsia) and fresh injuries or surgeries. For multiple pregnancies or after IVF, I also recommend speaking with the treating physician before the first massage. If you experience discomfort, pain or contractions during a massage session, let me know immediately. The safety of you and your baby always has the highest priority. When in doubt, the rule is: ask your doctor, then have the massage.

frequency.title: How Often During Pregnancy?
frequency.description: In the second trimester, I recommend a session every 2-3 weeks. In the third trimester, weekly massage is possible, especially when complaints such as back pain or swelling increase. Many of my clients particularly appreciate the mobile massage - they don't have to travel to a studio and can rest directly afterwards. The ideal schedule is individual and depends on your complaints and your budget. Some clients begin in the fourth month with monthly sessions and increase the frequency to weekly in the last trimester. Others book only as needed when complaints increase. Generally, regular sessions are more effective than sporadic single appointments, as the body gets used to the treatment and the effects build on each other. The 60-minute session is ideal for most pregnant women, as it provides sufficient time for a thorough treatment without being too tiring.

positioning.title: Positioning during the massage
positioning.description: Proper positioning is particularly important during pregnancy massage for both safety and comfort. From the second trimester, the prone position is avoided for obvious reasons. Instead, I work mainly in the side-lying position, which is considered the safest and most comfortable position. The client lies on her side while special cushions support the belly, knees and head. This position relieves the lower back and allows optimal access to the back and hip muscles. During the session, we switch sides so both halves of the body can be treated. Short segments in the supine position are possible in the second trimester but are avoided from week 20 onwards, as the baby's weight can compress the large vena cava. In the semi-seated position with a slightly elevated upper body, the neck, shoulders and arms can be treated well. I bring all necessary cushions and positioning aids, so you don't need to prepare anything at home.

postpartum.title: Massage after birth
postpartum.description: The time after birth presents the body with new challenges. Massage can effectively support the recovery process. After a natural birth without complications, massage can be resumed after 1-2 weeks. After a caesarean section, I recommend waiting 4-6 weeks and obtaining the doctor's clearance. Postpartum massage helps with muscle recovery, relieves tension from frequent breastfeeding and carrying the baby, and supports the hormonal balance in returning to equilibrium. Many new mothers develop neck and shoulder pain from breastfeeding and the constant downward gaze at the baby. The lower back also suffers from frequent lifting and carrying. Last but not least, massage contributes to emotional well-being and can counteract postpartum exhaustion. Mobile massage is ideal during this phase, as you don't have to leave your newborn or transport them. You can have the massage while a partner or another caregiver looks after the baby.

mobileAdvantage.title: Why mobile massage is ideal during pregnancy
mobileAdvantage.description: Especially during pregnancy, mobile massage offers decisive advantages over visiting a studio. The most obvious: you don't have to drive. Particularly in the third trimester, driving can be uncomfortable, and public transport with a large belly is exhausting. Then there is the time saving, which is especially valuable during pregnancy when energy is limited. After the massage, you can lie down and rest directly without having to make the journey home. The relaxation effect is thereby fully preserved. In your own surroundings, it is also easier to relax. You know every smell, every sound and feel safe. During pregnancy, when the senses are particularly sensitive, this is a great advantage. The privacy of your own home is especially important to many pregnant women. Should you need to use the bathroom during or after the massage (which happens frequently during pregnancy), your own bathroom is just a few steps away. I bring all necessary equipment and leave your room exactly as I found it.

mobileBenefits.title: Benefits of Mobile Massage
mobileBenefits.intro: More and more people in Zurich are choosing mobile massage instead of studio visits. The reasons are compelling. Mobile massage combines professional quality with maximum comfort. You save time, stay in your familiar surroundings and can make optimal use of the therapeutic effect of the massage. As a mobile massage therapist with distinction at the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I bring everything needed for a first-class treatment. In this guide, you will learn why mobile massage is the best choice for different life situations.

convenience.title: No Commute, Save Time
convenience.description: In Zurich, the journey to a massage studio alone can take 30-60 minutes, there and back. With a mobile massage, you save this time entirely. I come to you with a professional massage table, high-quality oils and all necessary equipment. Especially during heavy traffic and bad weather, this is an enormous advantage. Add up the journey there and back, the search for parking and the waiting time at the studio, and you quickly lose 60-90 minutes on top of your treatment time. With a 60-minute massage, you spend a total of 2 to 2.5 hours, even though only one hour of that is the actual massage. With mobile massage, the time invested equals the treatment time. You open the door, I set up in a few minutes, and the massage begins. Afterwards, I pack up and you can immediately enjoy your evening. For working professionals with a full schedule, this time saving is often the decisive reason for choosing mobile massage.

comfort.title: Relaxation in Familiar Surroundings
comfort.description: In your own flat, it is easier to relax completely. No unfamiliar environment, no unusual sounds, no waiting in a reception area. After the massage, you can go straight to the sofa or bed - the relaxation is not lost on the way home. The effect of a massage unfolds best when you can let go completely. In a studio, you have to get dressed again after the treatment, pay at the reception, drive home through traffic. All of this can undo the deep relaxation built up during the massage. At home, on the other hand, you can lie on the couch in your own bathrobe after the massage, listen to your favourite music and enjoy the warmth and suppleness of your muscles. Many of my clients report that they sleep better after a mobile massage than after a studio visit, because the relaxation is not interrupted by the journey home.

hygiene.title: Hygiene and Cleanliness
hygiene.description: I bring fresh towels, disinfected equipment and new oils to every session. You benefit from the hygiene of your own home combined with professional standards. After the massage, I leave your room just as I found it. My massage table is thoroughly disinfected after every use and fitted with fresh covers. The oils used are in sealed containers and freshly dispensed for each client. Towels are washed at 90 degrees and transported individually wrapped. Compared to some studios where multiple therapists use the same rooms and tables, with mobile massage you have the certainty that everything is fresh and prepared only for you. For clients with allergies or sensitive skin, I can adjust the oils and products individually. Simply let me know before the treatment.

flexibility.title: Flexible Scheduling
flexibility.description: Massage studios have fixed opening hours. I offer appointments from Monday to Sunday, 08:00 to 20:00, including evenings and weekends. Perfect for working professionals with a full schedule or families who need flexible times. Appointment changes are possible without fuss up to 24 hours beforehand. You don't have to work around a studio's opening hours or book weeks in advance. Spontaneous appointments are also possible subject to availability - simply contact me via WhatsApp or phone. The weekend flexibility is particularly valued: while many studios close early on Saturdays and are closed on Sundays, I offer the full time window on these days as well. For shift workers who are free at atypical times, mobile massage is often the only way to integrate professional massage treatments into their routine.

aftercare.title: Optimal Aftercare
aftercare.description: An often underestimated advantage: after a mobile massage, you can rest immediately. Drink a glass of water, lie down, fall asleep. No driving, no public transport, no journey home. The therapeutic effect of the massage can thus unfold optimally. In the 30-60 minutes after a massage, the most important regeneration processes take place in the body: circulation is elevated, muscles continue to relax, and stress hormones are broken down. If you are sitting in a car during this phase, battling through traffic, a large part of this effect is lost. At home, you can instead take a warm bath that enhances the massage's effect, or simply lie quietly. Evening massages in particular benefit from the possibility of going straight to bed afterwards. Many of my clients deliberately book the last appointment of the day and fall into a deep, restful sleep after the massage.

zurich.title: Mobile Massage in the Zurich Region
zurich.description: I offer mobile massage throughout Zurich, around Lake Zurich and in the surrounding cantons. From Kreis 1 to Kreis 12, from Meilen to Waedenswil, from Schwyz to Rapperswil - I come to you. As a prize winner of the Swiss Massage Championship 2026, I guarantee professional quality at the highest level, directly at your home. My service area covers the entire Lake Zurich region and surrounding municipalities. Whether you live in the city of Zurich, on the right or left shore of Lake Zurich, in the canton of Schwyz or in the Rapperswil-Jona region - I come to you. Travel is included in the price, there are no hidden additional costs. My location in Sattel (SZ) enables central and flexible accessibility to the entire region. If you have questions about coverage in your municipality, simply contact me via WhatsApp or phone.

families.title: Benefits for families with children
families.description: For parents with small children, visiting a massage studio is often a logistical challenge. Who looks after the children? How long will I be away? What if something happens? Mobile massage solves all these problems. You can book the massage while the children are sleeping, playing or being looked after by a partner. You are in the house and reachable at all times should anything come up. The time commitment is limited to the treatment time itself - no travel, no waiting area, no return journey. Especially for mothers on parental leave, mobile massage is ideal: you can plan the appointment flexibly around the baby's routine. It is no problem for older children either. They play in the next room while you enjoy your massage. Many of my clients with children say that without mobile massage, they would have to go without regular massage treatments because organising a studio visit would be too much effort. The massage can take place in any room that has enough space for the table, from the living room to the bedroom.

elderly.title: Benefits for older and mobility-impaired people
elderly.description: For older people or persons with limited mobility, the journey to a massage studio can be a considerable hurdle. Stairs, public transport, long walks from the car park - all of this can be difficult or even impossible. Mobile massage brings professional treatment directly to the living room. Many older clients particularly benefit from regular massage: it improves circulation, maintains mobility, alleviates joint complaints and reduces feelings of loneliness and isolation. Massage in familiar surroundings is often more relaxing for older people, as they feel safe and don't have to cope with unfamiliar places. I adapt the treatment individually, take health limitations into account and work particularly gently. Mobile massage is also a relief for caring relatives: they don't have to transport anyone and can use the gained time for other tasks. For people in assisted living or senior residences, I come directly to the facility. Get in touch with me - I will find a suitable solution.

corporate.title: Massage at the workplace
corporate.description: More and more companies in Zurich recognise the value of massage as an employee benefit. Mobile massage in the office is straightforward, effective and shows employees appreciation. Even short sessions of 20-30 minutes can release tension, boost concentration and lift team morale. I bring all the equipment and only need a quiet room - a meeting room or an empty office is sufficient. The massage can be organised as a one-off event or as a regular offering, for example a fixed day per week or month. Employees book their time slots individually, and the workflow is minimally disrupted. For companies, office massage has measurable benefits: fewer sick days due to back pain, higher employee satisfaction and a positive employer brand. Demand is especially high in industries with a lot of screen work, such as IT, finance or media. Contact me for a tailored offer for your company. I am happy to prepare a proposal that suits your team size and budget.

meta.description: Professional Swedish massage at your home in Zurich. Deep relaxation, recovery, improved circulation. 120 CHF (60 min) • 160 CHF (90 min). Book now!

benefits.title: Benefits of Swedish Massage
benefits.1: Deep relaxation of muscles and nervous system
benefits.2: Improved blood circulation and tissue nourishment
benefits.3: Reduced stress and inner tension levels
benefits.4: Improved sleep quality and overall well-being

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
The session begins with a brief discussion of your current concerns and preferences. I then work with the five classic Swedish massage techniques: effleurage (gentle stroking), petrissage (kneading), friction, tapotement (tapping), and vibration. The treatment follows a proven sequence, from the back through the legs to the arms and neck.

The pressure is individually adjusted to your sensitivity. Most clients find the massage pleasantly relaxing with a soothing deep effect. After the session, you'll feel light and released. Mild muscle soreness the next day is possible but rare, Swedish massage is gentler than sports massage.

I use high-quality, natural massage oils without artificial fragrances. For sensitive skin or allergies, I adjust the products accordingly. I bring my professional massage table, you only need to prepare two large towels.

Many clients notice a clear improvement in tension and sleep quality after just the first session. For lasting results with chronic issues, I recommend a cycle of 4-6 sessions.

idealFor.title: Who is Swedish Massage for?
idealFor.description: Ideal for people with stressful lifestyles, sedentary office work, or chronic tension. Especially recommended for those seeking regular body care and recovery. Also perfect as an introduction for massage beginners.

frequency.title: How often should you get Swedish Massage?
frequency.description: For optimal results, I recommend 1-2 sessions per week over 4-6 weeks. For maintenance, one session every 2-4 weeks is sufficient. For acute issues, a more intensive frequency may be beneficial.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Drink plenty of water before your massage. Avoid heavy meals at least one hour before. After the massage: rest for 10-15 minutes, drink plenty of water, and avoid intensive physical activity for the rest of the day.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Swedish Massage
contraindications.intro: Swedish massage is safe for most people. However, you should consult your doctor or avoid massage in these cases:
items: Acute inflammation, fever, or infectious diseases
Open wounds, skin rashes, or fresh scars in the treatment area
Severe cardiovascular diseases or uncontrolled high blood pressure
Thrombosis or increased risk of blood clots
Recent surgeries or bone fractures
Malignant tumours in the treatment area
contraindications.note: When in doubt, please consult your doctor. Inform me before treatment about any existing conditions or medications.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Swedish Massage

items.question: What's the difference between Swedish and Classical massage?
items.answer: Swedish massage uses five specific techniques in a set sequence and focuses particularly on relaxation. Classical massage is more flexible in technique choice and can target individual problem areas more specifically.

items.question: Do I need to undress completely?
items.answer: No. You can keep your underwear on. During the massage, only the area being treated is uncovered. Your comfort and privacy are top priority.

items.question: How long does the effect last?
items.answer: The relaxation typically lasts 3-7 days. With regular sessions, every 1-2 weeks, a cumulative effect builds up that permanently reduces tension and improves wellbeing.

items.question: Can I exercise after the massage?
items.answer: I recommend avoiding intensive physical activity for 24 hours after the massage. Light movement like walking is beneficial and supports the effect.

items.question: Is Swedish massage painful?
items.answer: No, Swedish massage is one of the gentlest massage types. The pressure is adjusted to your sensitivity. If anything feels uncomfortable, let me know at any time.

items.question: Why is Oleg Swiss champion in Swedish massage?
items.answer: I won 1st place at the 5th Swiss Massage Championship 2026 in the Swedish Massage category. This competition evaluates technique, rhythm, pressure control, and overall quality, confirming the highest professional level.

benefits.title: Benefits of Classical Massage
benefits.1: Relieves muscle tension and spasms
benefits.2: Improves blood circulation and lymph flow
benefits.3: Promotes deep relaxation
