whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
The classical massage begins with gentle tissue warming through stroking movements. Gradually, I transition to deeper techniques, kneading, rubbing, and targeted pressure point work. Each muscle group is systematically treated: back, shoulders, neck, arms, and legs. I continuously adjust the intensity based on your feedback.

The treatment feels soothing and releasing. At particularly tense spots, you may feel brief, pleasant pressure pain, this is a sign that the tension is releasing. After the massage, you'll feel relaxed and more mobile. Mild muscle soreness the following day is normal with severe tension and subsides quickly.

I work with professional massage oils tailored to your skin type. When needed, I also use warming oils for particularly tense areas. All equipment, massage table, oils, fresh covers, I bring with me.

Many clients report noticeable relief from neck and back pain after just the first session. For chronic issues, the full effect becomes apparent after 3-4 regular sessions.

idealFor.title: Who is Classical Massage for?
idealFor.description: Suitable for almost everyone: from office workers with back pain to seniors with limited mobility. Particularly effective for general fatigue, tension headaches, and sleep problems.

frequency.title: How often should you get Classical Massage?
frequency.description: For prevention, I recommend one session every 2-3 weeks. For chronic issues, 1-2 sessions per week for 4-8 weeks is ideal. The frequency can then be reduced to monthly.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Come relaxed, avoid stress and rushing before your session. Wear comfortable clothing. After the session: take it easy, drink water, and take a warm bath to enhance the effects.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Classical Massage
contraindications.intro: Classical massage is suitable for almost everyone. However, please consult your doctor in these cases:
items: Acute inflammation, fever, or flu-like symptoms
Skin conditions, open wounds, or fresh scars
Severe circulatory problems or uncontrolled high blood pressure
Advanced thrombosis or varicose veins
Fresh injuries, sprains, or bone fractures
First trimester of pregnancy (specialised pregnancy massage recommended)
contraindications.note: Please inform me of all known conditions and medications before treatment. If unsure, consult your GP.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Classical Massage

items.question: Who is classical massage best suited for?
items.answer: For practically everyone, from office workers with neck tension to seniors with limited mobility. It's the most versatile massage type and a perfect introduction for massage newcomers.

items.question: How often should I come?
items.answer: For prevention, one session every 2-3 weeks is sufficient. For acute issues, I recommend 1-2 sessions per week initially for 4-6 weeks, then monthly maintenance sessions.

items.question: What if I feel pain during the massage?
items.answer: Speak up immediately. Mild pressure pain at tense spots is normal, but strong pain should never occur. I adjust the intensity at any time based on your feedback.

items.question: Does classical massage help with headaches?
items.answer: Yes, especially tension headaches. Tension in the neck, shoulders, and upper back muscles are common triggers. By releasing these tensions, the frequency and intensity of headaches can be significantly reduced.

items.question: Should I eat before the massage?
items.answer: Don't eat anything heavy for at least an hour before. A light snack is fine. Drink plenty of water before and especially after treatment.

items.question: What's the difference between 60 and 90 minutes?
items.answer: At 60 minutes, I focus on the main problem areas (back, neck, shoulders). At 90 minutes, all body areas can be thoroughly treated, ideal for a full-body relaxation.

benefits.title: Benefits of Sports Massage
benefits.1: Accelerates muscle recovery after training
benefits.2: Improves flexibility and range of motion
benefits.3: Prevents injuries and overuse
benefits.4: Enhances athletic performance

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
As a former Olympic gymnast, I understand the needs of a strained body from personal experience. The sports massage begins with an analysis of your current training load and problem areas. Then I work specifically with deep tissue techniques, trigger point therapy, and fascial work on the affected muscle groups.

Sports massage is more intense than a classical massage. At tense spots, the pressure may be felt as brief, releasing pain, I communicate constantly with you and adjust the intensity. After the treatment, your muscles feel released and more mobile. Mild soreness the next day is normal and shows that the deep layers were reached.

I use special sports massage oils that can have warming or cooling effects, depending on your needs. For acute injuries, I work without oil using targeted mobilisation techniques. My massage table is suitable for all body sizes and weight classes.

The effect is immediately noticeable: more mobility and less tension. For optimal athletic performance, I recommend regular sessions as a fixed part of your training plan, before competitions for preparation, afterwards for recovery.

idealFor.title: Who is Sports Massage for?
idealFor.description: For athletes of all levels, from hobby sportspeople to professionals. Also ideal for those with physically demanding jobs. Particularly effective for muscle soreness, limited mobility, and injury prevention.

frequency.title: How often should you get Sports Massage?
frequency.description: Before competitions: 2-3 days before. After intensive training: within 24-48 hours. Regular athletes benefit from weekly sessions. During rest periods, every 2-3 weeks is sufficient.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Don't schedule your massage right after training, wait at least 2 hours. Tell me about any current injuries or pain. After the massage: light stretching and plenty of water. Avoid intensive training for 24 hours.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Sports Massage
contraindications.intro: Sports massage is intense and not suitable in every condition. Caution is advised in these cases:
items: Acute muscle injuries (strains, tears) within the first 48-72 hours
Acute inflammation, swelling, or bruising
Fever or acute infectious diseases
Recent surgeries in the treatment area
Thrombosis or deep vein thrombosis
Severe exhaustion or overtraining, relaxation massage is better suited here
contraindications.note: If unsure after an injury, please consult your sports physician or physiotherapist first.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Sports Massage

items.question: Is sports massage painful?
items.answer: Sports massage is more intense than classical massage. At tense spots, a brief releasing pressure pain may occur, the so-called 'good pain'. But strong pain should never occur. I always work within your comfort zone.

items.question: When should I book massage before a competition?
items.answer: Ideally 2-3 days before. A massage directly on competition day can over-relax the muscles. The day before, a short 30-minute stimulating massage is possible.

items.question: How soon after training can I get a massage?
items.answer: Wait at least 2 hours after intense training. The body needs time to start the acute inflammation process. The optimal time for recovery massage is 6-24 hours after training.

items.question: Which sports benefit most?
items.answer: All sports benefit from sports massage. I most frequently treat runners, cyclists, CrossFit athletes, swimmers, and strength athletes. It's also ideal for desk workers with active leisure time.

items.question: Can sports massage prevent injuries?
items.answer: Yes. Regular sports massage improves muscle elasticity, releases hardened areas early, and identifies overload zones before they lead to injuries. Many professional athletes use it as a fixed part of their injury prevention programme.

items.question: Why is a former Olympic gymnast the right sports massage therapist?
items.answer: Through my career as an Olympic gymnast, I understand the demands of high-performance sport first-hand. I know which muscle groups are particularly stressed by which activities and how to optimally recover them.

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
The anti-cellulite massage uses intensive, dynamic techniques that specifically target the deep tissue layers. I work with vigorous kneading, rolling, and suction movements that activate lymph flow and strongly stimulate circulation in the affected areas. Typical treatment zones are thighs, buttocks, abdomen, and upper arms.

The massage is more intense than a relaxation massage, you'll feel noticeable pressure and vigorous tissue work. This is intentional and necessary for the effect. The skin may be slightly reddened after treatment, which subsides within a few hours. Bruising is rare with proper technique.

I use special anti-cellulite oils with circulation-boosting ingredients. Additionally, I use cupping glasses when needed to enhance the effect. Between sessions, I recommend drinking plenty of water and light exercise.

First results, a feeling of lightness and firmer skin, are noticeable after the very first session. Visible changes in skin appearance show after 6-8 sessions. For optimal results, I recommend a course of 10-12 sessions combined with healthy nutrition and exercise.

idealFor.title: Who is Anti-Cellulite Massage for?
idealFor.description: For women and men who want to improve their skin texture, reduce water retention, and firm their body. Especially effective combined with healthy nutrition and regular exercise.

frequency.title: How often should you get Anti-Cellulite Massage?
frequency.description: For visible results, I recommend a course of 8-12 sessions, 2-3 times per week. Then for maintenance, 1-2 sessions per month. Initial improvements are often noticeable after just 3-4 sessions.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Drink plenty of water before and after treatment, this supports detoxification. Avoid caffeine and alcohol on the day of your massage. Regular exercise between sessions significantly enhances the effect.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Anti-Cellulite Massage
contraindications.intro: Anti-cellulite massage is an intense treatment. It is not suitable in these cases:
items: Varicose veins or spider veins in the treatment area
Skin conditions, eczema, or psoriasis at treated areas
Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Blood-thinning medication
Recent surgeries, scars, or inflammation in the treatment area
Severe cardiovascular diseases or lymphoedema
contraindications.note: Cellulite is a cosmetic concern, not a medical problem. Consult your doctor if unsure.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Anti-Cellulite Massage

items.question: Will cellulite disappear completely through massage?
items.answer: Massage can significantly improve the appearance of cellulite but not eliminate it entirely. Cellulite has genetic and hormonal causes. Massage improves circulation, lymph flow, and skin elasticity, combined with exercise and nutrition, the best results are achievable.

items.question: Will I get bruises?
items.answer: With proper technique, bruising is rare. Mild redness after treatment is normal and fades within a few hours. Let me know if you tend to bruise easily.

items.question: How many sessions do I need for visible results?
items.answer: First improvements (firmer skin, less water retention) are noticeable after 3-4 sessions. Clearly visible results show after 8-10 sessions. I recommend a course of 10-12 sessions, 2-3 times per week.

items.question: Can I support the results at home?
items.answer: Yes! Drink 2-3 litres of water daily, reduce salt and sugar, exercise regularly, and use a dry brush for circulation. These measures significantly enhance the massage effect.

items.question: Is the massage also suitable for men?
items.answer: Absolutely. Men can also benefit from anti-cellulite massage, especially for reducing water retention, improving skin elasticity, and activating metabolism.

meta.title: Pregnancy Massage Zurich | Safe Relaxation for Expectant Mothers
meta.description: Gentle pregnancy massage at your home in Zurich. Relieves back pain, reduces swelling, improves sleep. 120 CHF (60 min). Safe from the 2nd trimester onwards.

benefits.title: Benefits of Pregnancy Massage
benefits.1: Relieves back and leg pain
benefits.3: Improves sleep quality and well-being
benefits.4: Promotes relaxation and connection with baby

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
Your safety and wellbeing come first. The pregnancy massage is performed exclusively in a comfortable side-lying position, I use special positioning cushions that optimally support your belly, back, and legs. This allows you to fully relax without any pressure on your abdomen.

The techniques are gentle and flowing, with special focus on typical problem areas during pregnancy: lower back, hips, swollen legs, and tense shoulders. The pressure is noticeably lighter than a classical massage. No pressure points that could trigger contractions are stimulated. The massage is safe from the second trimester onwards.

I exclusively use natural, fragrance-free oils without essential additives, safe for you and your baby. All products are dermatologically tested and hypoallergenic. Please inform me about any special sensitivities or complications.

Most expectant mothers report immediate relief from back pain and swollen legs. Sleep often improves after just the first session. Regular massages in the third trimester can make the overall pregnancy experience noticeably more comfortable.

idealFor.title: Who is Pregnancy Massage for?
idealFor.description: For expectant mothers from the second trimester onwards. Helps with typical pregnancy complaints such as back pain, swollen legs, and sleep problems. Please consult your doctor first if you have a high-risk pregnancy.

frequency.title: How often should you get Pregnancy Massage?
frequency.description: In the second trimester: every 2-3 weeks. In the third trimester: weekly if desired. The massage is individually adapted to the respective stage of pregnancy.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Let me know which week of pregnancy you are in. Eat a light snack before the massage. I use special cushions for optimal comfort in the side-lying position. Rest after the massage.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Pregnancy Massage
contraindications.intro: Pregnancy massage is safe from the second trimester. However, it is not recommended in these cases:
items: First trimester (up to week 12)
High-risk pregnancy or doctor-ordered bed rest
Premature contractions or bleeding
Pre-eclampsia or pregnancy-related high blood pressure
Placenta praevia or other placental complications
Fever, infections, or acute illness
contraindications.note: Always discuss massage with your gynaecologist or midwife, especially with complications. Bring medical clearance if unsure.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Pregnancy Massage

items.question: From which week of pregnancy is massage safe?
items.answer: From the second trimester (week 13). In the first trimester, the risk of complications is elevated, so I recommend starting from week 13. In the third trimester up to shortly before the due date, massage is particularly beneficial.

items.question: In which position is the massage performed?
items.answer: Exclusively in a comfortable side-lying position. I use special positioning cushions for belly, back, and legs. Lying on your stomach is neither necessary nor possible. Side-lying is the safest and most comfortable position.

items.question: Which oils do you use?
items.answer: Exclusively natural, fragrance-free oils without essential additives. Essential oils like rosemary, peppermint, or sage are deliberately avoided during pregnancy. All products are dermatologically tested.

items.question: Can the massage trigger contractions?
items.answer: No. I deliberately avoid all pressure points and techniques associated with triggering contractions (e.g., certain points on ankles and hands). The techniques used are gentle and safe.

items.question: Does the massage help with swollen legs?
items.answer: Yes, very effectively. Gentle massage techniques and lymphatic drainage can significantly reduce swelling in legs and feet. Many pregnant women report immediate relief after a session.

items.question: Can my partner be present during the massage?
items.answer: Of course. Your partner is very welcome. On request, I also show simple massage techniques your partner can use at home, especially for the lower back.

meta.title: Children's Massage Zurich | Loving Health Care
meta.description: Children's massage at your home in Zurich. Promotes healthy development, improves sleep and digestion. Playful and gentle. For babies and children of all ages.

benefits.title: Benefits of Children's Massage
benefits.1: Supports muscle and bone development
benefits.3: Strengthens immune system and health
benefits.4: Promotes emotional bonding and security

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
Children's massage is not a scaled-down adult massage, it's an entirely different approach. The session starts playfully: I get to know your child, build trust, and explain everything in a child-friendly way. Only when your child feels comfortable do I begin with gentle, rhythmic stroking and kneading movements.

All techniques are extremely gentle and performed at your child's pace, without pressure and without stress. If your child becomes restless or needs a break, we adapt. The massage should be a positive experience, not an obligation. You as a parent are present throughout the entire session and can observe and learn the techniques.

I exclusively use child-friendly, hypoallergenic oils without fragrances. With babies, I often work without oil, using only warm hands. The massage surface is set up on the floor or bed, wherever your child feels most comfortable.

Children often respond faster than adults: better sleep and more relaxed behaviour are often visible after the very first session. I also show you simple techniques you can use at home, making massage a daily ritual between you and your child.

idealFor.title: Who is Children's Massage for?
idealFor.description: For babies from 3 months and children of all ages. Especially helpful for sleep problems, digestive issues, motor development delays, and for hyperactive children who need relaxation.

frequency.title: How often should Children's Massage be done?
frequency.description: For babies: 2-3 short sessions per week. For older children: 1-2 times per week. I also teach you techniques that you can use at home to continue the massage between appointments.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Make sure your child is not hungry or overtired. The massage takes place in a warm, quiet room. You can be present throughout the session. Bring your child's favorite toy.

contraindications.title: When children's massage is not suitable
contraindications.intro: Children's massage is very gentle, but in some cases it should be postponed or avoided:
items: Fever, cold, or acute infectious diseases
Skin rashes, open wounds, or eczema in the treatment area
Immediately after vaccinations (wait 24-48 hours)
Acute abdominal pain of unclear origin
Bone fractures or fresh injuries
When the child explicitly refuses, massage should always be voluntary
contraindications.note: For chronic conditions or developmental disorders, please consult the paediatrician first. I adapt the treatment individually to your child's needs.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Children's Massage

items.question: From what age is children's massage possible?
items.answer: From about 3 months for baby massage. For older children, there's no age limit. Techniques and duration are age-appropriate, from 15-20 minutes for babies to 45-60 minutes for teenagers.

items.question: Do I need to be present as a parent?
items.answer: Yes, a parent should always be present with children. This gives your child security and you can observe the techniques and apply them at home.

items.question: What if my child can't stay still?
items.answer: That's completely normal! Especially with younger children, I adapt, we take breaks, use toys as distraction, or change position. There's no pressure. The massage should be fun.

items.question: Does massage help with sleep problems?
items.answer: Yes, that's one of the most common reasons for children's massage. Regular gentle massage before bedtime can significantly shorten the time to fall asleep and improve sleep quality, especially for restless or hyperactive children.

items.question: Can I learn the massage techniques myself?
items.answer: Yes, that's actually encouraged! During each session, I show you simple techniques you can use at home. This makes massage a daily bonding ritual between you and your child.

meta.title: Cupping Massage Zurich | Traditional Therapy for Modern Problems
meta.description: Cupping massage at your home in Zurich. Relieves back pain, fights cellulite, improves circulation. Can be added to any massage (15 min).

benefits.title: Benefits of Cupping Massage
benefits.1: Activates intensive circulation and regeneration
benefits.2: Relieves chronic back and neck pain
benefits.3: Effectively fights cellulite and water retention
benefits.4: Releases deep muscle tension and trigger points

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
During cupping, I place special vacuum cupping glasses on the skin, which lift the tissue through negative pressure. This intensely stimulates circulation in the deeper tissue layers and releases even stubborn tension that is difficult to reach with conventional massage. The glasses are either placed statically on trigger points or guided dynamically over larger muscle areas.

The sensation during cupping is unusual but not painful, most clients describe it as an intense pulling that quickly subsides. After treatment, characteristic red to purple circles typically appear on the skin. These discolourations are completely normal, harmless, and disappear within 3-7 days. They are not a sign of injury but indicate activated circulation.

For the cupping massage, I use high-quality medical cupping glasses in various sizes along with massage oil for gliding. The treatment can be booked as a standalone 15-minute session or as a supplement to any other massage.

The effect is often immediately noticeable: tension releases, and the affected area feels warm and well-circulated. For chronic issues, I recommend 3-4 sessions at 1-2 week intervals. Plan your appointments so that the marks don't coincide with important occasions.

idealFor.title: Who is Cupping Massage for?
idealFor.description: For people with chronic back and neck pain, muscle tension, and circulation problems. Also effective for cellulite and general detoxification. Not suitable for those on blood thinners or with sensitive skin.

frequency.title: How often should you get Cupping Massage?
frequency.description: As a complement to massage: possible at every session. As a standalone treatment: every 1-2 weeks. The marks (red circles) disappear after 3-7 days, plan accordingly.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Drink plenty of water before and after treatment. Be aware that cupping can leave temporary discolorations, wear covering clothing if needed. Avoid hot showers for 24 hours after treatment.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Cupping
contraindications.intro: Cupping is an effective therapy but not suitable for everyone. Avoid in these cases:
items: Blood-thinning medication (Aspirin, Warfarin, etc.)
Haemophilia or other blood clotting disorders
Sunburn, skin conditions, or open wounds in the treatment area
Pregnancy (especially on abdomen and lower back)
Varicose veins in the treatment area
Very thin or sensitive skin (e.g., from cortisone use)
contraindications.note: The discolourations from cupping are normal and not a sign of injury. Plan important appointments accordingly, as marks remain visible for 3-7 days.

items.question: Are the red circles after cupping dangerous?
items.answer: No, the discolourations are harmless. They result from increased blood flow under the skin and disappear completely in 3-7 days. The colour intensity shows how severe the circulation disturbance in the tissue was.

items.question: Does cupping hurt?
items.answer: Most clients describe it as an unusual but not painful pulling sensation. The negative pressure is noticeable initially but becomes pleasant after a few seconds. I reduce pressure on sensitive areas.

items.question: Can I combine cupping with massage?
items.answer: Yes, that's actually the most popular option! 15 minutes of cupping as a supplement to a classical or sports massage. Cupping pre-releases deep tension, and the subsequent massage works it out.

items.question: How often can I have cupping?
items.answer: Once the marks from the last session have faded, typically every 1-2 weeks. For acute issues, frequency can be higher initially, in consultation with me.

items.question: Can I shower after cupping?
items.answer: Wait at least 2-3 hours and avoid hot water for 24 hours. Lukewarm showering is fine. Also avoid sauna and swimming pools for 24-48 hours after treatment.

meta.title: Relaxation Massage Zurich | Pure Recovery for Body and Mind
meta.description: Relaxation massage at your home in Zurich. Stress relief, mental calm, complete relaxation. Perfect after a long day. 120 CHF (60 min) • 160 CHF (90 min).

benefits.title: Benefits of Relaxation Massage
benefits.1: Reduces stress and mental strain
benefits.3: Brings inner peace and clarity
benefits.4: Strengthens emotional well-being

whatToExpect.title: What to Expect from a Session
whatToExpect.description:
The relaxation massage is a timeout for body and mind. The session begins with a few quiet moments, I dim the lights on request and play gentle relaxation music. Then I work with long, flowing strokes and gentle pressure across the entire body. The rhythm is steady and meditative.

This massage is intentionally gentle, no deep pressure, no intense techniques. It's exclusively about relaxation and letting go. Many clients fall asleep during the treatment, this is expressly welcome. After the massage, you'll feel refreshed, centred, and inwardly calm. Muscle soreness does not occur with this type of massage.

I use aromatic massage oils with natural scents such as lavender, chamomile, or bergamot, according to your preference. For scent sensitivity, I work with neutral oil. The entire atmosphere is designed for maximum relaxation.

The effect is immediately noticeable: stress reduction, mental calm, and a feeling of deep contentment. Regular sessions, weekly or every two weeks, can demonstrably lower stress hormones, regulate blood pressure, and permanently improve sleep quality.

idealFor.title: Who is Relaxation Massage for?
idealFor.description: For everyone who wants to reduce stress and find inner peace. Especially recommended after long work weeks, when at risk of burnout, for sleep disorders, or simply as a regular self-care ritual.

frequency.title: How often should you get Relaxation Massage?
frequency.description: For maximum anti-stress effect: weekly or biweekly. As a self-care ritual: monthly. Many clients experience the best results with a regular routine.

preparation.title: Before and after tips
preparation.description: Give yourself time after the massage, don't schedule stressful appointments afterwards. Dim the lights and play soft music to enhance the atmosphere. Put your phone on silent and enjoy complete peace.

contraindications.title: When to avoid Relaxation Massage
contraindications.intro: Relaxation massage is the gentlest type and suitable for almost everyone. Still, there are few exceptions:
items: Acute inflammation, fever, or infectious diseases
Open wounds or contagious skin conditions
Immediately after surgeries (medical clearance required)
Severe cardiovascular diseases without medical clearance
Acute thrombosis or deep vein thrombosis
Severe mental health crises (seek professional help first)
contraindications.note: Relaxation massage is complementary, not a substitute for medical or psychological treatment. If unsure, ask your doctor.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Relaxation Massage

items.question: What if I fall asleep during the massage?
items.answer: That's absolutely welcome! Falling asleep is a sign of complete relaxation. I'll gently wake you at the end of the session. Many clients report that massage naps are some of their best sleep.

items.question: Can relaxation massage help with burnout?
items.answer: Yes, as a complementary measure. Regular relaxation massage demonstrably lowers cortisol (stress hormone) and increases serotonin and dopamine. It doesn't replace professional burnout therapy but can effectively support it.

items.question: Should I be quiet or can we talk?
items.answer: Both are perfectly fine! Some clients relax best in silence, others prefer to chat. There's no right or wrong, do what feels natural for you.

items.question: Can I choose the music?
items.answer: Of course! I bring relaxation music, but you're welcome to play your own playlist. Some clients prefer nature sounds or complete silence, anything goes.

items.question: How does relaxation massage differ from Swedish?
items.answer: Relaxation massage uses exclusively gentle, flowing techniques and focuses on the overall experience (atmosphere, scents, music). Swedish massage follows a more structured protocol with specific techniques and can also use deeper pressure.

items.question: Is a relaxation massage suitable as a gift?
items.answer: Absolutely, it's one of the most popular gifts! I can issue a personalised gift voucher. Many gift regular monthly sessions as a special token of appreciation.
